## Entry 186
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:03:02.572Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:03:02.572Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_sKQ0XqykMKNgPbcc5AnRDOp2",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: server/app/services/agent_proxy.py\n@@\n CHAT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30.0\n \n \n+def _auth_headers(settings: Settings) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    token = settings.agent_internal_token.strip()\n+    if not token:\n+        raise ValueError(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for agent requests\")\n+    return {\"X-Internal-Token\": token}\n+\n+\n def _extract_message_from_events(events: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n@@\n async def _ensure_session(\n     client: httpx.AsyncClient,\n     base_url: str,\n     app_name: str,\n     user_id: str,\n     session_id: str,\n+    headers: dict[str, str],\n ) -> None:\n     response = await client.post(\n         f\"{base_url}/apps/{app_name}/users/{user_id}/sessions/{session_id}\",\n+        headers=headers,\n         json=None,\n     )\n@@\n async def get_chat_response(settings: Settings, session_id: str, message: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n+    headers = _auth_headers(settings)\n     async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=CHAT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) as client:\n         base_url = settings.agent_server_url.rstrip(\"/\")\n         app_name = settings.agent_app_name\n         user_id = \"metrosense\"\n         await _ensure_session(\n             client=client,\n             base_url=base_url,\n             app_name=app_name,\n             user_id=user_id,\n             session_id=session_id,\n+            headers=headers,\n         )\n         response = await client.post(\n             f\"{base_url}/run\",\n+            headers=headers,\n             json={\n                 \"appName\": app_name,\n                 \"userId\": user_id,\n                 \"sessionId\": session_id,\n                 \"newMessage\": {\"role\": \"user\", \"parts\": [{\"text\": message}]},\n@@\n async def get_agent_health(settings: Settings) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    headers = _auth_headers(settings)\n     async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\n-        response = await client.get(f\"{settings.agent_server_url.rstrip('/')}/health\")\n+        response = await client.get(\n+            f\"{settings.agent_server_url.rstrip('/')}/health\",\n+            headers=headers,\n+        )\n         response.raise_for_status()\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_sKQ0XqykMKNgPbcc5AnRDOp2",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: server/app/services/agent_proxy.py\n@@\n CHAT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30.0\n \n \n+def _auth_headers(settings: Settings) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    token = settings.agent_internal_token.strip()\n+    if not token:\n+        raise ValueError(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for agent requests\")\n+    return {\"X-Internal-Token\": token}\n+\n+\n def _extract_message_from_events(events: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n@@\n async def _ensure_session(\n     client: httpx.AsyncClient,\n     base_url: str,\n     app_name: str,\n     user_id: str,\n     session_id: str,\n+    headers: dict[str, str],\n ) -> None:\n     response = await client.post(\n         f\"{base_url}/apps/{app_name}/users/{user_id}/sessions/{session_id}\",\n+        headers=headers,\n         json=None,\n     )\n@@\n async def get_chat_response(settings: Settings, session_id: str, message: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n+    headers = _auth_headers(settings)\n     async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=CHAT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) as client:\n         base_url = settings.agent_server_url.rstrip(\"/\")\n         app_name = settings.agent_app_name\n         user_id = \"metrosense\"\n         await _ensure_session(\n             client=client,\n             base_url=base_url,\n             app_name=app_name,\n             user_id=user_id,\n             session_id=session_id,\n+            headers=headers,\n         )\n         response = await client.post(\n             f\"{base_url}/run\",\n+            headers=headers,\n             json={\n                 \"appName\": app_name,\n                 \"userId\": user_id,\n                 \"sessionId\": session_id,\n                 \"newMessage\": {\"role\": \"user\", \"parts\": [{\"text\": message}]},\n@@\n async def get_agent_health(settings: Settings) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    headers = _auth_headers(settings)\n     async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\n-        response = await client.get(f\"{settings.agent_server_url.rstrip('/')}/health\")\n+        response = await client.get(\n+            f\"{settings.agent_server_url.rstrip('/')}/health\",\n+            headers=headers,\n+        )\n         response.raise_for_status()\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

## Entry 201
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:03:19.360Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:03:19.360Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat <<'PY' > agents/app/proxy.py\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport os\\nfrom typing import Iterable\\n\\nimport httpx\\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI, Header, HTTPException, Request, Response\\n\\napp = FastAPI(title=\\\"MetroSense Agents Proxy\\\")\\n\\nADK_BASE_URL = os.getenv(\\\"ADK_BASE_URL\\\", \\\"http://127.0.0.1:8021\\\").rstrip(\\\"/\\\")\\nINTERNAL_TOKEN = os.getenv(\\\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\\\", \\\"\\\").strip()\\n\\n\\ndef _require_internal_token(header_value: str | None) -> None:\\n    if not INTERNAL_TOKEN:\\n        raise RuntimeError(\\\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for the agents proxy\\\")\\n    if not header_value or header_value != INTERNAL_TOKEN:\\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=\\\"Invalid internal token\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef _filtered_request_headers(headers: Iterable[tuple[str, str]]) -> dict[str, str]:\\n    blocked = {\\\"host\\\", \\\"content-length\\\", \\\"connection\\\", \\\"x-internal-token\\\"}\\n    return {key: value for key, value in headers if key.lower() not in blocked}\\n\\n\\ndef _filtered_response_headers(headers: Iterable[tuple[str, str]]) -> dict[str, str]:\\n    blocked = {\\n        \\\"content-length\\\",\\n        \\\"connection\\\",\\n        \\\"transfer-encoding\\\",\\n        \\\"content-encoding\\\",\\n    }\\n    return {key: value for key, value in headers if key.lower() not in blocked}\\n\\n\\n@app.get(\\\"/health\\\")\\nasync def health() -> dict[str, str]:\\n    return {\\\"agent\\\": \\\"ok\\\"}\\n\\n\\n@app.api_route(\\\"/{path:path}\\\", methods=[\\\"GET\\\", \\\"POST\\\", \\\"PUT\\\", \\\"PATCH\\\", \\\"DELETE\\\", \\\"OPTIONS\\\"])\\nasync def proxy(\\n    request: Request,\\n    path: str,\\n    x_internal_token: str | None = Header(default=None, alias=\\\"X-Internal-Token\\\"),\\n) -> Response:\\n    _require_internal_token(x_internal_token)\\n    target_url = f\\\"{ADK_BASE_URL}/{path}\\\"\\n    body = await request.body()\\n    headers = _filtered_request_headers(request.headers.items())\\n\\n    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:\\n        response = await client.request(\\n            request.method,\\n            target_url,\\n            params=request.query_params,\\n            content=body,\\n            headers=headers,\\n        )\\n\\n    response_headers = _filtered_response_headers(response.headers.items())\\n    return Response(\\n        content=response.content,\\n        status_code=response.status_code,\\n        headers=response_headers,\\n        media_type=response.headers.get(\\\"content-type\\\"),\\n    )\\nPY\"}",
    "call_id": "call_aYB82ovZo72tKoU0Sm7WFAFG"
  }
}
```

## Entry 216

**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:03:32.976Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat <<'SH' > agents/scripts/start_agents.sh\\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\\nset -euo pipefail\\n\\nadk_port=\\\"${ADK_PORT:-8021}\\\"\\nproxy_port=\\\"${PROXY_PORT:-8020}\\\"\\n\\nuv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port \\\"$adk_port\\\" . &\\nadk_pid=$!\\n\\nuv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port \\\"$proxy_port\\\" &\\nproxy_pid=$!\\n\\ntrap 'kill \\\"$adk_pid\\\" \\\"$proxy_pid\\\"' INT TERM\\n\\nwait -n \\\"$adk_pid\\\" \\\"$proxy_pid\\\"\\nSH\"}",
    "call_id": "call_jbvy6MshWHIZ1JmBi71R132f"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat <<'SH' > agents/scripts/start_agents.sh\\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\\nset -euo pipefail\\n\\nadk_port=\\\"${ADK_PORT:-8021}\\\"\\nproxy_port=\\\"${PROXY_PORT:-8020}\\\"\\n\\nuv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port \\\"$adk_port\\\" . &\\nadk_pid=$!\\n\\nuv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port \\\"$proxy_port\\\" &\\nproxy_pid=$!\\n\\ntrap 'kill \\\"$adk_pid\\\" \\\"$proxy_pid\\\"' INT TERM\\n\\nwait -n \\\"$adk_pid\\\" \\\"$proxy_pid\\\"\\nSH\"}",
    "call_id": "call_jbvy6MshWHIZ1JmBi71R132f"
  }
}
```

```json
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
```
```json
    "call_id": "call_600hRyV7q6Qz6RFqCW53vPuj",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docker-compose.yml\n@@\n   api:\n     build: ./server\n     environment:\n       ENV: development\n       DATABASE_URL: postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@db:5432/app_scaffold\n+      AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN: ${AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN}\n     ports:\n       - \"8000:8000\"\n     depends_on:\n       - db\n@@\n   agents:\n     build: ./agents\n     environment:\n       GOOGLE_API_KEY: ${GOOGLE_API_KEY:-}\n       GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI: ${GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI:-false}\n       AGENT_MODEL: ${AGENT_MODEL:-gemini-2.0-flash}\n+      AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN: ${AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN}\n     ports:\n       - \"8020:8020\"\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

## Entry 248
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:03:54.928Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:03:54.928Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_JZadw0teSfTo9P970jElwM6a",
    "output": "Chunk ID: ebd1a3\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 339\nOutput:\n- `app.services` depends on `app.db`, `app.core` only (must NOT import from `app.api`)\n- `app.db` depends on `app.core` only (must NOT import from `app.services` or `app.api`)\n- Enforced by `import-linter` in CI\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Start PostgreSQL\ndocker-compose up -d db\n\n# Backend\ncd server\nuv sync --all-extras\nuv run alembic upgrade head\nuv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010\n\n# Agents (separate terminal)\ncd agents\nuv sync\nexport GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_key\nuv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020 .\n\n# Optional: ADK Web UI (separate terminal)\ncd agents\nuv run adk web --port 8001\n\n# Frontend (separate terminal)\ncd client\npnpm install\npnpm run dev\n```\n\nApp runs at http://localhost:5173 with API proxied to :8010.\n\n## Quality Gates\n\nBackend (from `server/`):\n```bash\nuv run ruff format --check .    # Formatting\nuv run ruff check .             # Linting\nuv run mypy .                   # Type checking (strict)\nuv run lint-imports             # Module boundaries\nuv run pytest tests/unit -q     # Unit tests\nuv run pytest tests/integration -q  # Integration tests\n```\n\nFrontend (from `client/`):\n```bash\npnpm run lint   # ESLint\npnpm run build  # TypeScript + Vite build\n```\n\nFrontend E2E (from `client/`):\n```bash\npnpm exec playwright install\npnpm run test:e2e\n```\n\n## API Endpoints\n\n| Method | Path | Auth | Description |\n"
  }
}
```

```json
    "output": "Chunk ID: ebd1a3\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 339\nOutput:\n- `app.services` depends on `app.db`, `app.core` only (must NOT import from `app.api`)\n- `app.db` depends on `app.core` only (must NOT import from `app.services` or `app.api`)\n- Enforced by `import-linter` in CI\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Start PostgreSQL\ndocker-compose up -d db\n\n# Backend\ncd server\nuv sync --all-extras\nuv run alembic upgrade head\nuv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010\n\n# Agents (separate terminal)\ncd agents\nuv sync\nexport GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_key\nuv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020 .\n\n# Optional: ADK Web UI (separate terminal)\ncd agents\nuv run adk web --port 8001\n\n# Frontend (separate terminal)\ncd client\npnpm install\npnpm run dev\n```\n\nApp runs at http://localhost:5173 with API proxied to :8010.\n\n## Quality Gates\n\nBackend (from `server/`):\n```bash\nuv run ruff format --check .    # Formatting\nuv run ruff check .             # Linting\nuv run mypy .                   # Type checking (strict)\nuv run lint-imports             # Module boundaries\nuv run pytest tests/unit -q     # Unit tests\nuv run pytest tests/integration -q  # Integration tests\n```\n\nFrontend (from `client/`):\n```bash\npnpm run lint   # ESLint\npnpm run build  # TypeScript + Vite build\n```\n\nFrontend E2E (from `client/`):\n```bash\npnpm exec playwright install\npnpm run test:e2e\n```\n\n## API Endpoints\n\n| Method | Path | Auth | Description |\n"
  }
}
```

```json
    "output": "Chunk ID: 65cb7a\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 472\nOutput:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020 .`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n- Current backend tests cover health behavior and auth/password scaffolding.\n- Current integration tests cover health and auth flow scaffolding.\n- As chat features are added, extend coverage for:\n  - backend chat contracts\n  - backend-to-agent coordination\n  - agent response parsing/transport\n  - frontend rendering of streamed text, `RiskCard`, and `Artifact` payloads\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- It is acceptable to document `agents/` as planned architecture, but label it clearly as planned until the folder, runtime, and wiring are added.\n- If current scaffold behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 257
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:04:20.901Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:04:20.901Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_AQSTjnG8pXy3702sqN1vmJKa",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: README.md\n@@\n # Backend\n cd server\n uv sync --all-extras\n uv run alembic upgrade head\n+export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here\n uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010\n \n-# Agents (separate terminal)\n+# Agents (separate terminals)\n cd agents\n uv sync\n export GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_key\n-uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020 .\n+export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here\n+uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .\n+\n+# Agents proxy (separate terminal)\n+cd agents\n+export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here\n+uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020\n \n # Optional: ADK Web UI (separate terminal)\n cd agents\n uv run adk web --port 8001\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_AQSTjnG8pXy3702sqN1vmJKa",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: README.md\n@@\n # Backend\n cd server\n uv sync --all-extras\n uv run alembic upgrade head\n+export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here\n uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010\n \n-# Agents (separate terminal)\n+# Agents (separate terminals)\n cd agents\n uv sync\n export GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_key\n-uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020 .\n+export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here\n+uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .\n+\n+# Agents proxy (separate terminal)\n+cd agents\n+export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here\n+uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020\n \n # Optional: ADK Web UI (separate terminal)\n cd agents\n uv run adk web --port 8001\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

## Entry 262
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:04:34.026Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:04:34.026Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_LfF8Ye54OZKTyCQt5ZSzx5eh",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: AGENTS.md\n@@\n - Local startup today:\n   - `docker-compose up -d db`\n-  - `cd server && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n-  - `cd agents && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020 .`\n+  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n+  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n+  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=your-internal-token-here && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n   - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n   - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

## Entry 277
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:04:49.561Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:04:49.561Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_pUCNJIESxiUMXokjk2nIa6IA",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: server/tests/integration/agent_stub.py\n@@\n-from fastapi import FastAPI\n+import os\n+\n+from fastapi import FastAPI, Header, HTTPException\n from pydantic import BaseModel\n \n app = FastAPI(title=\"Agent Stub\")\n+INTERNAL_TOKEN = os.getenv(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\", \"\").strip()\n@@\n @app.post(\"/apps/{app_name}/users/{user_id}/sessions/{session_id}\")\n-async def create_session(app_name: str, user_id: str, session_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:\n+async def create_session(\n+    app_name: str,\n+    user_id: str,\n+    session_id: str,\n+    x_internal_token: str | None = Header(default=None, alias=\"X-Internal-Token\"),\n+) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    _require_token(x_internal_token)\n     return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n@@\n-@app.post(\"/run\")\n-async def run_agent(_: RunRequest) -> list[dict]:\n+@app.post(\"/run\")\n+async def run_agent(\n+    _: RunRequest,\n+    x_internal_token: str | None = Header(default=None, alias=\"X-Internal-Token\"),\n+) -> list[dict]:\n+    _require_token(x_internal_token)\n     return [\n@@\n         }\n     ]\n+\n+\n+def _require_token(header_value: str | None) -> None:\n+    if not INTERNAL_TOKEN:\n+        raise RuntimeError(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for agent stub\")\n+    if header_value != INTERNAL_TOKEN:\n+        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=\"Invalid internal token\")\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**

```json
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: server/tests/integration/agent_stub.py\n@@\n-from fastapi import FastAPI\n+import os\n+\n+from fastapi import FastAPI, Header, HTTPException\n from pydantic import BaseModel\n \n app = FastAPI(title=\"Agent Stub\")\n+INTERNAL_TOKEN = os.getenv(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\", \"\").strip()\n@@\n @app.post(\"/apps/{app_name}/users/{user_id}/sessions/{session_id}\")\n-async def create_session(app_name: str, user_id: str, session_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:\n+async def create_session(\n+    app_name: str,\n+    user_id: str,\n+    session_id: str,\n+    x_internal_token: str | None = Header(default=None, alias=\"X-Internal-Token\"),\n+) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    _require_token(x_internal_token)\n     return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n@@\n-@app.post(\"/run\")\n-async def run_agent(_: RunRequest) -> list[dict]:\n+@app.post(\"/run\")\n+async def run_agent(\n+    _: RunRequest,\n+    x_internal_token: str | None = Header(default=None, alias=\"X-Internal-Token\"),\n+) -> list[dict]:\n+    _require_token(x_internal_token)\n     return [\n@@\n         }\n     ]\n+\n+\n+def _require_token(header_value: str | None) -> None:\n+    if not INTERNAL_TOKEN:\n+        raise RuntimeError(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for agent stub\")\n+    if header_value != INTERNAL_TOKEN:\n+        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=\"Invalid internal token\")\n*** End Patch"
```

```json
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat <<'PY' > server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport pytest\\n\\nfrom app.core.config import Settings\\nfrom app.services import agent_proxy\\n\\n\\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\\nasync def test_agent_proxy_requires_internal_token() -> None:\\n    settings = Settings(agent_internal_token=\\\"\\\")\\n    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\\\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\\\"):\\n        await agent_proxy.get_agent_health(settings)\\nPY\"}",
    "call_id": "call_rHoIfB4UczsnIcO063DLhHAa"
  }
}
```

```json
{
```

```json
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
```
```json
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Updating proxy config and env examples**\n\nI've updated the agents and server `.env.example` files to include the proxy port (8021) and ensured the health endpoint in the proxy is correctly handled without requiring headers. The docker-compose setup uses default ports consistently, and the `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN` is added to integration test settings. The docs were updated for the internal token, and the env and proxy settings are aligned for clarity and correctness."
  }
}
```

## Entry 286
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:05:16.627Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:05:16.627Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Planning proxy health endpoint to reflect backend status**\n\nI'm deciding to replace the static `/health` response in the proxy with a forwarded call to the ADK `/health` endpoint to accurately reflect agent health. This will keep `/health` public without requiring headers but still return 503 if the ADK is down, improving health check correctness for the backend."
  }
}
```

## Entry 290
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:05:16.820Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:05:16.820Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_KK4ir5YpbGRZNteVL3hXq4IS",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 690e3d\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 488\nOutput:\n\ndef _error_response(code: str, message: str, status_code: int) -> JSONResponse:\n    return JSONResponse(\n        status_code=status_code,\n        content={\"error\": ErrorPayload(code=code, message=message).model_dump()},\n    )\n\n\n@router.post(\n    \"/chat\",\n    response_model=ChatResponse,\n    responses={\n        502: {\"description\": \"Agent unavailable\"},\n        504: {\"description\": \"Agent timeout\"},\n    },\n)\nasync def chat(\n    payload: ChatRequest,\n    app_settings: Settings = Depends(settings),\n) -> ChatResponse | JSONResponse:\n    try:\n        response_payload = await agent_proxy.get_chat_response(\n            settings=app_settings,\n            session_id=payload.session_id,\n            message=payload.message,\n        )\n    except httpx.TimeoutException:\n        return _error_response(\n            code=\"AGENT_TIMEOUT\",\n            message=\"The agent service did not respond in time.\",\n            status_code=status.HTTP_504_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT,\n        )\n    except (httpx.HTTPError, ValueError):\n        return _error_response(\n            code=\"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\",\n            message=\"The agent service is currently unavailable.\",\n            status_code=status.HTTP_502_BAD_GATEWAY,\n        )\n\n    return ChatResponse.model_validate(response_payload)\n\n\n@router.get(\"/health\", response_model=CompositeHealthResponse)\nasync def api_health(app_settings: Settings = Depends(settings)) -> CompositeHealthResponse:\n    try:\n        payload = await agent_proxy.get_agent_health(app_settings)\n    except httpx.HTTPError:\n        payload = {\"backend\": \"ok\", \"agent\": \"down\", \"status\": \"degraded\"}\n\n    return CompositeHealthResponse.model_validate(payload)\n\n\n@router.delete(\"/chat/{session_id}\", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)\nasync def clear_chat_session(\n    session_id: str,  # noqa: ARG001 - placeholder until agents service supports reset\n    app_settings: Settings = Depends(settings),  # noqa: ARG001\n) -> None:\n    return None\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 293
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:05:24.279Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:05:24.279Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_F7BAd2eV0DTqcV1uy4MbuJSx",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: agents/app/proxy.py\n@@\n-@app.get(\"/health\")\n-async def health() -> dict[str, str]:\n-    return {\"agent\": \"ok\"}\n+@app.get(\"/health\")\n+async def health() -> Response:\n+    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\n+        response = await client.get(f\\\"{ADK_BASE_URL}/health\\\")\n+    response_headers = _filtered_response_headers(response.headers.items())\n+    return Response(\n+        content=response.content,\n+        status_code=response.status_code,\n+        headers=response_headers,\n+        media_type=response.headers.get(\\\"content-type\\\"),\n+    )\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

```json
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: agents/app/proxy.py\n@@\n-@app.get(\"/health\")\n-async def health() -> dict[str, str]:\n-    return {\"agent\": \"ok\"}\n+@app.get(\"/health\")\n+async def health() -> Response:\n+    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\n+        response = await client.get(f\\\"{ADK_BASE_URL}/health\\\")\n+    response_headers = _filtered_response_headers(response.headers.items())\n+    return Response(\n+        content=response.content,\n+        status_code=response.status_code,\n+        headers=response_headers,\n+        media_type=response.headers.get(\\\"content-type\\\"),\n+    )\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

## Entry 296

**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:05:38.569Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Updating agent health header handling and related configs**\n\nThe agent health endpoint now always adds a header, requiring updates to server tests and the agent proxy service to handle this consistently. Environment examples and README updates reflect the new ADK_BASE_URL and local port distinctions for the backend and ADK services. Integration tests and startup scripts adjust to token expectations and process management changes accordingly."
  }
}
```

## Entry 297
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:05:38.570Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:05:38.570Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Fixing env token loading timing in agent stub tests**\n\nThe agent stub test currently reads the internal token as a constant at import time, causing it to miss env updates made later in fixtures. Updating the stub to fetch the token dynamically inside the request handler will ensure it uses the correct environment settings during tests."
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:05:40.485Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_oJm1ZO1RX0O5921P8cHTt8lF",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: server/tests/integration/agent_stub.py\n@@\n-import os\n-\n from fastapi import FastAPI, Header, HTTPException\n from pydantic import BaseModel\n \n app = FastAPI(title=\"Agent Stub\")\n-INTERNAL_TOKEN = os.getenv(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\", \"\").strip()\n@@\n def _require_token(header_value: str | None) -> None:\n-    if not INTERNAL_TOKEN:\n+    internal_token = os.getenv(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\", \"\").strip()\n+    if not internal_token:\n         raise RuntimeError(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for agent stub\")\n-    if header_value != INTERNAL_TOKEN:\n+    if header_value != internal_token:\n         raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=\"Invalid internal token\")\n*** End Patch"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:05:44.386Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_70mw6vd0ePAEw1o5fdCqatTm",
    "output": "Chunk ID: fb633d\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 358\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI, Header, HTTPException\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\n\napp = FastAPI(title=\"Agent Stub\")\n\n\nclass RunRequest(BaseModel):\n    appName: str\n    userId: str\n    sessionId: str\n    newMessage: dict | None = None\n    streaming: bool = False\n\n\n@app.get(\"/health\")\nasync def health() -> dict[str, str]:\n    return {\"agent\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.post(\"/apps/{app_name}/users/{user_id}/sessions/{session_id}\")\nasync def create_session(\n    app_name: str,\n    user_id: str,\n    session_id: str,\n    x_internal_token: str | None = Header(default=None, alias=\"X-Internal-Token\"),\n) -> dict[str, str]:\n    _require_token(x_internal_token)\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.post(\"/run\")\nasync def run_agent(\n    _: RunRequest,\n    x_internal_token: str | None = Header(default=None, alias=\"X-Internal-Token\"),\n) -> list[dict]:\n    _require_token(x_internal_token)\n    return [\n        {\n            \"content\": {\n                \"role\": \"model\",\n                \"parts\": [{\"text\": \"Stubbed response from agent.\"}],\n            }\n        }\n    ]\n\n\ndef _require_token(header_value: str | None) -> None:\n    internal_token = os.getenv(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\", \"\").strip()\n    if not internal_token:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for agent stub\")\n    if header_value != internal_token:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=\"Invalid internal token\")\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 334
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:06:35.195Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:06:35.195Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Summary**\n\n- Added a shared secret header to the backend agent proxy in `agents/app/proxy.py`.\n- Created a new `agents/app/` directory with an `__init__.py` and proxy script.\n- Added the new agent start script at `agents/scripts/start_agents.sh`.\n- Updated environment examples in `agents/.env.example` and `server/.env.example` to reflect necessary variables.\n- Updated documentation and Docker configuration to mention port 8021 for local setup.\n\n**Tests**\n\n- Tests have not been run.\n\n**Next Steps**\n\n- Set the `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN` environment variable in both `server` and `agents` environments as well as in Docker configurations.\n- Run agents using the new start script at `agents/scripts/start_agents.sh`.\n\n**Notes**\n\n- The `agents/uv.lock` file was not updated and may require attention."
  }
}
```

If you want, I can update it once you confirm network access.\n\n**Next Steps**\n1. Set `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN` in both backend and agents envs (local and docker).\n2. Run ADK on `8021` and the proxy on `8020` (see updated `README.md` and `AGENTS.md`).",
```json
    "phase": null
  }
}
```

## Entry 341
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:07:38.219Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:07:38.219Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "message",
    "role": "developer",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "input_text",
        "text": "<collaboration_mode># Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only, concise by default, and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nWhen possible, prefer a compact structure with 3-5 short sections, usually: Summary, Key Changes or Implementation Changes, Test Plan, and Assumptions. Do not include a separate Scope section unless scope boundaries are genuinely important to avoid mistakes.\n\nPrefer grouped implementation bullets by subsystem or behavior over file-by-file inventories. Mention files only when needed to disambiguate a non-obvious change, and avoid naming more than 3 paths unless extra specificity is necessary to prevent mistakes. Prefer behavior-level descriptions over symbol-by-symbol removal lists. For v1 feature-addition plans, do not invent detailed schema, validation, precedence, fallback, or wire-shape policy unless the request establishes it or it is needed to prevent a concrete implementation mistake; prefer the intended capability and minimum interface/behavior changes.\n\nKeep bullets short and avoid explanatory sub-bullets unless they are needed to prevent ambiguity. Prefer the minimum detail needed for implementation safety, not exhaustive coverage. Within each section, compress related changes into a few high-signal bullets and omit branch-by-branch logic, repeated invariants, and long lists of unaffected behavior unless they are necessary to prevent a likely implementation mistake. Avoid repeated repo facts and irrelevant edge-case or rollout detail. For straightforward refactors, keep the plan to a compact summary, key edits, tests, and assumptions. If the user asks for more detail, then expand.\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n</collaboration_mode>"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

```json
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository currently contains a working full-stack scaffold: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The intended product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer\n- Treat the current scaffold as the implementation baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
```

```json
        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only, concise by default, and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nWhen possible, prefer a compact structure with 3-5 short sections, usually: Summary, Key Changes or Implementation Changes, Test Plan, and Assumptions. Do not include a separate Scope section unless scope boundaries are genuinely important to avoid mistakes.\n\nPrefer grouped implementation bullets by subsystem or behavior over file-by-file inventories. Mention files only when needed to disambiguate a non-obvious change, and avoid naming more than 3 paths unless extra specificity is necessary to prevent mistakes. Prefer behavior-level descriptions over symbol-by-symbol removal lists. For v1 feature-addition plans, do not invent detailed schema, validation, precedence, fallback, or wire-shape policy unless the request establishes it or it is needed to prevent a concrete implementation mistake; prefer the intended capability and minimum interface/behavior changes.\n\nKeep bullets short and avoid explanatory sub-bullets unless they are needed to prevent ambiguity. Prefer the minimum detail needed for implementation safety, not exhaustive coverage. Within each section, compress related changes into a few high-signal bullets and omit branch-by-branch logic, repeated invariants, and long lists of unaffected behavior unless they are necessary to prevent a likely implementation mistake. Avoid repeated repo facts and irrelevant edge-case or rollout detail. For straightforward refactors, keep the plan to a compact summary, key edits, tests, and assumptions. If the user asks for more detail, then expand.\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"

    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository currently contains a working full-stack scaffold: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The intended product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer\n- Treat the current scaffold as the implementation baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - ## Entry 355
```
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T18:12:47.990Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T18:12:47.990Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cd8f3-94f5-75f1-9ce4-8140925d9b07",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-10",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "plan",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only, concise by default, and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nWhen possible, prefer a compact structure with 3-5 short sections, usually: Summary, Key Changes or Implementation Changes, Test Plan, and Assumptions. Do not include a separate Scope section unless scope boundaries are genuinely important to avoid mistakes.\n\nPrefer grouped implementation bullets by subsystem or behavior over file-by-file inventories. Mention files only when needed to disambiguate a non-obvious change, and avoid naming more than 3 paths unless extra specificity is necessary to prevent mistakes. Prefer behavior-level descriptions over symbol-by-symbol removal lists. For v1 feature-addition plans, do not invent detailed schema, validation, precedence, fallback, or wire-shape policy unless the request establishes it or it is needed to prevent a concrete implementation mistake; prefer the intended capability and minimum interface/behavior changes.\n\nKeep bullets short and avoid explanatory sub-bullets unless they are needed to prevent ambiguity. Prefer the minimum detail needed for implementation safety, not exhaustive coverage. Within each section, compress related changes into a few high-signal bullets and omit branch-by-branch logic, repeated invariants, and long lists of unaffected behavior unless they are necessary to prevent a likely implementation mistake. Avoid repeated repo facts and irrelevant edge-case or rollout detail. For straightforward refactors, keep the plan to a compact summary, key edits, tests, and assumptions. If the user asks for more detail, then expand.\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response.
```
